items.title: ICF vs. VNQI: Which Real Estate ETF Is Setup for Better Returns in 2026 and Beyond?
items.summary: The article compares two real estate ETFs: iShares Select U.S. REIT ETF (ICF), which offers concentrated exposure to 30 U.S. REITs with strong AI-driven data center holdings, and Vanguard Global ex-U.S. Real Estate ETF (VNQI), which provides diversified international real estate exposure with lower costs and higher dividend yields. ICF has outperformed VNQI over the past year due to AI infrastructure investments, while VNQI offers better diversification and cost efficiency.

items.title: AI’s Hidden Cost Problem Is Making Memory Chip Makers the Biggest Winners
items.summary: As AI infrastructure buildout accelerates, memory chip suppliers (Micron, Samsung, SK Hynix) have become critical bottlenecks, commanding pricing power similar to oil producers during an airline boom. While hyperscalers absorb rising memory costs to maintain competitive advantage, the economics remain unsustainable long-term. The AI profit pool is fragmenting, with memory suppliers capturing disproportionate gains until efficiency improvements, better monetization, or new supply capacity arrives.

items.title: You Can Buy 1 Share of Costco for Less Than $1,000 Right Now. Here's Why the Price Could Go Up by the End of 2030.
items.summary: Despite trading at premium valuation ratios (48x trailing earnings and 48x free cash flow), Costco's stock is positioned for continued growth through 2030. The warehouse club's 92.2% membership renewal rate, high-margin membership fee income, planned expansion of 30+ warehouses annually, and successful tech investments justify its premium pricing compared to discount retail peers.

items.title: SMH vs. SOXX vs. SOXQ: Which Semiconductor ETF Is the Best Buy Right Now?
items.summary: Three major semiconductor ETFs—SMH, SOXX, and SOXQ—compete in the sector as tech giants plan $700 billion in capex spending for 2026. SMH offers the most concentrated mega-cap exposure with strong historical returns, SOXX provides more diversification but at higher cost, while SOXQ delivers similar diversification to SOXX at nearly half the expense ratio, making it the recommended choice for cost-conscious investors.

items.title: Bargain Alert: This Tech Stock's Bold AI Strategy Could Unlock Explosive Upside
items.summary: Meta Platforms is investing heavily in AI infrastructure and developing its own MTIA chip program. While current AI spending appears substantial, the custom chip strategy could provide long-term efficiency gains and cost advantages across ranking, recommendations, advertising, and generative AI workloads, potentially transforming the margin outlook.

items.title: 3 Under-the-Radar Ways to Play Goldman's $1 Trillion AI Spending 2027 Forecast
items.summary: Goldman Sachs forecasts AI infrastructure spending could reach $920 billion to $1.4 trillion in 2027, up from $700 billion in 2026. The article identifies three stocks positioned to benefit: Alphabet (with cost advantages via TPUs), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC, with manufacturing monopoly), and ASML (sole provider of EUV lithography technology for chip production).
